messages.content:
ive been having some fun trying to visualize / group the problems:
[image.png](/user_uploads/5/53/mmRvt8bo030653VWTDfgf2AL/image.png)
this is the 1k normal set, I "fingerprint" the equations using a set of 150 randomly generated magmas with s2, s3, and s4. Once I do that it combines and maps it into 50 dim space, clusters it, and then projects it to 3d space for visualization 

heres a 50k sample that I did the fit on using 5k points for compute efficiency

[image.png](/user_uploads/5/cd/oBESc6DFfco1R3im1ILNTuXz/image.png)
the fuzziness in the points is increasing the size for better viewing as the # of points is quite small, im doing a 1 mil point run and will update with more pics / filters
i also very much feel out of my depth cause majority of my background is CS / basic ml, not abstract mathematics lol. i love data vis / data competitions though
I had a theory that if you could track the progress of the accuracy through the clusters you could literally "see" blind spots in the LLM's knowledge
should be a 50/50 true false split

@_**Het S|121** [said](https://zulip.sair.foundation/#narrow/channel/13-Math-Distillation-Challenge---equational-theories/topic/visualization/near/961):
```quote
[image.png](/user_uploads/5/e4/vXyz5PHe1SVRxOsjdONg7VWD/image.png)
redid with 700k points, interesting distributions
[image.png](/user_uploads/5/1f/UQlCjWJBCJO27M2XRgPfCu0C/image.png)
```

that's quite beautiful. if i give you a new magmas dataset would you be able to visualize its distribution too? i have been working on curating a "super hard" yet maximally distributed dataset of magmas for evals.

[image.png](/user_uploads/5/94/SrQSTqZ8reycLIA4KJ181QGf/image.png)
heres the "boundary" colors, yellow is on the boundary / edge of the clusters and purple / black is in the center, yellow should be harder problems

@_**stokarz|50** [said](https://zulip.sair.foundation/#narrow/channel/13-Math-Distillation-Challenge---equational-theories/topic/visualization/near/963):
````quote

```

that's quite beautiful. if i give you a new magmas dataset would you be able to visualize its distribution too? i have been working on curating a "super hard" yet maximally distributed dataset of magmas for evals.
````

I'd definitely be interested! send over the file and I can give it a shot

[image.png](/user_uploads/5/22/sm75NWrzRbAxG21yxIpyGYse/image.png)
heres what one of those strong clusters looks like when zoomed in, very convinient way of knowing that its actually doing something
it would be great to have an online visualization to dive in haha.
I can try and make a standalone html file from this, but it will probably be a little big lol
its really fun to navigate around it in 3D though, it looks a lot like neurons / neuron connections

[fp.html](/user_uploads/5/3e/AbE-xHLvz2DkoQHdgkCkbAyJ/fp.html)
testing a theory, does this work on yalls computers
promise its safe, it should be the 1000 normal problems visualized

[fp_50K.html](/user_uploads/5/f0/ou-8zB44Ao7Sz6a9EdkC7cgy/fp_50K.html)
heres a 50k sample, probably the largest i can upload to zulip
